fact: 3rd Space deliberately does not pay its performing/presenting guests (artists, musicians, actors). Jake Sacks explained the philosophy directly: 'We do not pay our guests and instead make it a shared space where you just ate with these people and now they get to show off their work and vision or whatever it looks like. The floor is theirs. Because once you pay a performer there is a hierarchy.' Note: the chef IS paid separately from ticket revenue.

Jake Sacks stated in a January 22, 2025 gen-zine.com interview: 'I want my value proposition to be rebuilding a community, leaning into the Medici effect idea of bringing together the right people and seeing what magic can come of it.' This framing is corroborated by the article's editorial context, which explicitly notes Sacks 'drawing inspiration from 15th-century Florence, where the Medici family's investment in artists led to a flourishing artistic community.' The philosophy remains actively expressed: 3rd Space's current Substack (© 2026) describes the brand as hosting curated dinners, and a separate interview (caseyraeborella.com) has Sacks stating his 'North Star is trying to find as many unique collaborations as I can bring together in the same room' — consistent with the Medici curation philosophy. The live 3rdspace.xyz homepage, while sparse (Careers page only), does not contradict this positioning.

Jake Sacks stated in a January 22, 2025 interview on gen-zine.com: 'Essentially, recreate SoHo House for the next generation, especially given the fact that SoHo House stopped accepting applications. There is a whole new market building up.' This competitive thesis is grounded in a real and confirmed Soho House policy: Soho House stopped admitting new members in its New York, London, and Los Angeles locations due to overcrowding, a move described as likely permanent. As of mid-2024, Soho House's global waitlist stood at an all-time high of 111,000, confirming the market gap Jake references is real and ongoing.

Jake Sacks (founder of 3rd Space) stated in an interview at caseyraeborella.com: 'I was fortunate enough to get funding from USC to host dinners for startup founders on campus, and then that project kind of blossomed into a passion of mine where I was like, what if I put a brand on top of this?' This origin story is corroborated by the 3rd Space About page (3rdspace3rdspace.xyz/about-us), which confirms the brand got its start in 2023 bringing together young startup founders on campus at USC, and by a January 2025 GEN-ZiNE article describing the USC Founder's Club as sponsored by the university and 5+ VC funds.

Jake Sacks of 3rd Space recounted: during a dinner event, the kitchen stopped working mid-service ('the stoves aren't heating'), so he left the building and went door to door to nearby apartments and restaurants asking to take over their kitchen, and within 15 minutes had relocated the chef to a new kitchen. This anecdote appears verbatim in a published interview on caseyraeborella.com.

"My friend Jake Sacks is on another level and I don't say that often." — third-party peer endorsement appearing in a Substack post at https://substack.com/home/post/p-157985337, authored by a fellow Substack writer referencing Jake Sacks, founder of 3rd Space.

3rd Space runs a careers page (careers.3rdspace.xyz) described as 'A list of curated roles from companies in the 3rd Space community. Many of these are off record, so the application links are emails of the founder. Tell them we sent you.'
